content:
# Seawater Intrusion

**Summary**: The movement of saline water into freshwater aquifers from the ocean.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] investigated seawater intrusion along the coastline based on variations in groundwater chemistry.

## Scientific Context

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] Identifies the impact of coastal proximity on groundwater salinity and quality.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] analyzed via variation in major ion concentrations (Na, Cl) and hydrogeochemical plots.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Seawater intrusion is a primary superimposed process affecting the coastal areas near Jubayl. ([[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992, p. 19]])

## Contrasting Values and Interpretations

- [[1992-hassan|Hassan, 1992]] Present near Jubayl.
